name: nextjs-devtools
description:
Next.js development tooling via MCP. Inspect routes, components, build info, and debug Next.js apps.
Use when working on Next.js applications, debugging routing, or inspecting app structure.
NOT for general React or non-Next.js projects.
capabilities: List all routes in a Next.js application including API routes
Get detailed information about specific routes including handlers and parameters
List all React components used in the application
Retrieve build configuration and optimization settings
Read next.config.js settings and environment variables
author: 92Bilal26
securitySummary: Legitimate Next.js development tooling. Static analyzer flagged Python f-strings as cryptographic patterns and subprocess.Popen as dangerous. These are false positives. The skill spawns a trusted MCP server package for Next.js inspection utilities.
valueStatement: Debugging Next.js applications is time-consuming without visibility into routes, components, and build configuration. This skill provides MCP-based tools to inspect app structure, list routes, examine components, and view build info directly from Claude.
limitations: Requires Next.js project with .next directory or after running next build
Does not modify or write to the Next.js application
Only works with Next.js projects, not standalone React apps
MCP server must be available via npx or running server process

useCases.title: Explore Unknown Next.js Projects
useCases.description: When joining a new Next.js codebase, use this skill to quickly understand the route structure, component organization, and build configuration without reading every file manually.

useCases.title: Debug Route Matching Issues
useCases.description: When routes are not matching as expected, use list-routes and get-route-info to verify which routes are registered and what handlers exist for specific paths.

useCases.title: Review Build Configuration
useCases.description: Before deployment or when troubleshooting build issues, inspect the actual build configuration being used including webpack settings, optimization flags, and environment variables.

promptTemplates.title: List All Application Routes
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the nextjs-devtools skill to list all routes in this Next.js application. Show me the route paths and their types (page, api, layout).

promptTemplates.title: Inspect Specific Route Details
promptTemplates.prompt: Use nextjs-devtools to get details about the route at /[path]. Show me the file handler, parameters, and any dynamic segment patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Find Component Usage
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the skill to list all React components in this application. Group them by where they are used (pages, layouts, or shared components).

promptTemplates.title: Check Build Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Run get-build-info and get-config to show me the current build configuration. Pay attention to webpack customizations, image optimization settings, and experimental features.

bestPractices: Run next build before inspecting build info to ensure .next directory exists
Start the MCP server once and reuse it for multiple queries to reduce startup overhead
Use get-route-info after list-routes to understand specific route handlers and parameters
antiPatterns: Using this skill on non-Next.js projects (will not find routes or build info)
Expecting real-time updates without restarting the MCP server after code changes
Running list-components on very large applications without filtering may produce extensive output

faq.question: What is Next.js DevTools?
faq.answer: Next.js DevTools is an MCP server that provides tools for inspecting Next.js applications. It can list routes, components, build configuration, and next.config.js settings.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: The skill uses npx to run next-devtools-mcp@latest automatically. No global installation is required. Ensure you have Node.js and npm installed.

faq.question: How does this skill connect to Next.js DevTools?
faq.answer: The skill includes an MCP client script that can spawn the Next.js DevTools server via npx (stdio transport) or connect to a running server via HTTP.

faq.question: What is the difference between stdio and HTTP transport?
faq.answer: Stdio spawns a new server process for each command. HTTP connects to a persistent server. HTTP is faster for multiple queries but requires starting the server first with start-server.sh.

faq.question: Why is my build info empty?
faq.answer: Build info requires the .next directory. Run next build in your project before using get-build-info. Some settings may not appear in development mode.

faq.question: Can this modify my Next.js application?
faq.answer: No. This skill is read-only. It only inspects routes, components, and configuration without making any changes to your application files.
